# llms.txt for theblas repository

## Site Summary
Title: theblas - A C++ BLAS Library Implementation
Description: A simple, educational implementation of Basic Linear Algebra Subprograms (BLAS) in C++. Includes core routines for vector and matrix operations, with CMake build system and unit tests.
Last Updated: 2026-03-22
Language: C++
License: See LICENSE file
Topics: BLAS, Linear Algebra, C++, Mathematics, High Performance Computing

## Page: README.md
Title: README - theblas
Summary: Project overview, build instructions, usage examples, and contribution guidelines for the theblas BLAS library.
Key Sections: Introduction, Building, Testing, Contributing

## Page: AGENTS.md
Title: AGENTS.md - AI Agent Documentation
Summary: Documentation for available AI agents in the workspace: Explore, CodeReviewer, Tester, DocWriter, SecurityAuditor, PerfAnalyzer. Includes usage patterns and maintenance notes.
Key Sections: Purpose, Agents (with descriptions), How to use

## Page: include/theblas/theblas.h
Title: Header File - theblas.h
Summary: Public API header for the theblas library, declaring BLAS functions like gemm, axpy, etc.
Key Sections: Function declarations, typedefs

## Page: src/theblas.cpp
Title: Implementation - theblas.cpp
Summary: C++ implementation of BLAS routines, optimized for clarity and performance.
Key Sections: Function definitions, algorithms

## Page: tests/test_theblas.cpp
Title: Unit Tests - test_theblas.cpp
Summary: Google Test-based unit tests for BLAS functions, ensuring correctness.
Key Sections: Test cases, fixtures

## Page: CMakeLists.txt
Title: Build Configuration - CMakeLists.txt
Summary: CMake build script for compiling the library and tests.
Key Sections: Project setup, targets, dependencies

## Page: CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md
Title: Contributor Covenant Code of Conduct
Summary: Community guidelines for respectful and inclusive contributions.
Key Sections: Pledge, Standards, Responsibilities, Scope, Enforcement

## Page: .github/skills/
Title: AI Skills Directory
Summary: Folder-based skill packages for AI workflows. Each skill lives at .github/skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md with discoverable metadata and task workflow guidance.
Key Sections: explore/SKILL.md, code-reviewer/SKILL.md, tester/SKILL.md, doc-writer/SKILL.md, security-auditor/SKILL.md, perf-analyzer/SKILL.md
